issues.title: usePaymentMethodsManager has no test file
issues.description: This hook orchestrates payment method CRUD operations (create, update, delete, reorder), manages expanded/collapsed UI state, and calls confirm() before deletion. It contains non-trivial business logic: sort-order swap on move up/down, auto-expand on create, and guard on move boundaries. None of this is covered by tests.
issues.fix: Create apps/payments/src/features/payment-methods/application/hooks/usePaymentMethodsManager.test.tsx. Cover: (1) handleCreate expands the new item on success; (2) handleDelete calls confirm and aborts if rejected; (3) handleMoveUp swaps sort_order with previous item; (4) handleMoveDown swaps sort_order with next item; (5) boundary guards (first/last item).

issues.title: useSupabaseAuth is duplicated across 5 apps with no test coverage
issues.description: useSupabaseAuth exists in apps/admin, apps/auth, apps/payments, apps/store, and apps/studio (5 copies), none have test files. While each may be structurally similar, differences in session handling or redirect logic between apps could silently diverge. The hook is security-critical (auth state management).
issues.fix: Add a test file for at least one representative app (e.g. apps/store/src/features/auth/application/hooks/useSupabaseAuth.test.ts). Cover: (1) returns authenticated user from session; (2) returns null when not authenticated; (3) sign-in/sign-out mutations call Supabase client correctly.

issues.title: getInitials util has no test file
issues.description: getInitials extracts up to 2-character initials from a name or falls back to the email's first character. It has distinct branches (name present, name absent, empty email) and a MAX_INITIALS_LENGTH cap that are worth verifying explicitly.
issues.fix: Create apps/admin/src/features/users/application/utils/getInitials.test.ts. Cover: (1) single-word name returns 1 uppercase char; (2) two-word name returns 2 initials; (3) name with 3+ words is capped at 2 chars; (4) null name falls back to email first char; (5) null name + empty email returns '?'.

issues.title: useClearCartCookie has no test (trivial wrapper — low priority)
issues.description: The hook is a one-liner that returns the clearCartCookie function directly. Its behavior is entirely covered by testing clearCartCookie itself. A dedicated hook test adds little value but is noted for completeness.
issues.fix: Consider testing clearCartCookie directly via its own infrastructure test rather than wrapping it here.

issues.title: apps/playground hooks not tested (playground — intentionally relaxed)
issues.description: The playground app follows relaxed quality expectations per monorepo-architecture.md. No test requirement applies here.
issues.fix: No action required — playground is an incubation sandbox.

issues.title: usePaymentMethodsManager: handleDelete uses globalThis.confirm — difficult to test without spy setup
issues.description: Using globalThis.confirm in a hook couples business logic to browser dialog APIs, making unit tests require vi.stubGlobal('confirm', ...) setup. Consider extracting the confirmation step to a prop callback (onDeleteConfirm) or a React dialog to make the hook testable without stubs.
issues.fix: Replace globalThis.confirm(t('deleteConfirm')) with an optional prop or context-provided confirmation function. This also improves DIP compliance — the hook would depend on an abstraction rather than the browser confirm dialog.

issues.title: No edge-case tests for loading/error states in useRecentActivity (already has test file)
issues.description: The test file exists (added in previous pass). Verify it covers the error state (API returning 500) and the empty-array case, as these are common dashboard edge cases that affect UX.
issues.fix: Ensure useRecentActivity.test.tsx includes: (1) isError = true path; (2) empty data array path rendering empty state.

issues.title: usePaymentMethodsManager sort-order swap logic is a bug-prone edge case worth specific test coverage
issues.description: handleMoveUp and handleMoveDown swap sort_order values by calling updateMutation.mutate twice. If the first mutation succeeds but the second fails, the list becomes inconsistent. The boundary guards (index === 0, index === methods.length - 1) are also easy to get wrong.
issues.fix: In the usePaymentMethodsManager test, mock updateMutation and assert it is called exactly twice with the correct swapped sort_order values. Also verify no mutation is called when at the boundary.

patterns.pattern: useSupabaseAuth duplicated across all 6 apps without any test
patterns.observation: Every app has its own copy of useSupabaseAuth with no test coverage. If this hook is structurally identical, it should be moved to packages/shared or packages/auth to reduce duplication and centralise testing. If app-specific differences exist, each variant should be tested independently.
patterns.recommendation: Consider consolidating into packages/auth (already listed in monorepo structure) and adding a single shared test.

patterns.observation: useClearCartCookie and usePaymentMethodsManager reference browser globals directly, violating DIP and making unit tests harder to write.
patterns.recommendation: Extract confirmation dialogs to component-level or via a passed callback to keep hooks pure.
